title: Affine interpolation is additive in endpoint data

source.citation: Alfio Quarteroni, Riccardo Sacco, and Fausto Saleri, Numerical Mathematics, 2nd ed., Springer, 2007, Ch. 8 (Polynomial Interpolation).
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: Affine interpolation of pointwise-added endpoint data equals the sum of the two affine interpolants.

declarations.name: affineInterp
declarations.statement: def affineInterp (theta y0 y1 : Real) : Real := (1 - theta) * y0 + theta * y1

declarations.name: affineInterp_add
declarations.statement: theorem affineInterp_add (theta y0 y1 z0 z1 : Real) : affineInterp theta (y0 + z0) (y1 + z1) = affineInterp theta y0 y1 + affineInterp theta z0 z1

tasks.input: Translate the interpolation theorem `affineInterp_add` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem affineInterp_add (theta y0 y1 z0 z1 : Real) : affineInterp theta (y0 + z0) (y1 + z1) = affineInterp theta y0 y1 + affineInterp theta z0 z1

tasks.input: Identify the main algebraic or order lemma needed for the interpolation proof.
